summary.intent: Implement data display components (Meter, Stat, Progress) for the ars-ui project and get them merged through code review.
summary.outcome: PR #682 was opened, Codex review identified 5 bugs (controlled-mode sync, non-finite math, aria-valuenow clamping), all were fixed with failing tests added first, and a follow-up commit was pushed to the branch.

repo: Controlled/uncontrolled mode switching in ars-core's Bindable requires sync_controlled(None) or reconstruction — not a simple flag toggle
Coverage gate uses --lib flag (cargo llvm-cov --lib), which only measures unit tests inside modules, not integration tests in tests/
The project uses cargo xtask for spec validation and snapshot linting (cargo xtask spec validate, cargo xtask lint snapshot-count)
PR labels include 'snapshot-reviewed' for PRs that add .snap fixtures, applied before triggering Codex review
Codex review is triggered via '@codex review' comment; 👀 reaction signals active review, 👍 signals approval
The review-loop skill polls every 2 minutes checking reactions, review threads, and CI status checks via GitHub GraphQL API

code.finding: Progress component was not normalizing non-finite values (NaN/infinity) before computing percent or emitting aria-valuenow, causing invalid attribute output
Meter component similarly lacked sanitization of non-finite range values and did not clamp exposed aria value attributes

Integration tests added: meter_root_clamps_and_sanitizes_value_attrs, progress_root_clamps_and_sanitizes_value_now, progress_set_props_preserves_value_control_mode_transitions to guard the review fixes

workflow: Add failing tests before patching when responding to code review — confirms the bug exists and the fix is targeted
cargo test does not accept multiple lib filter arguments in a single invocation; run separate commands per filter
Integration tests (tests/ dir) do not count toward --lib coverage; need equivalent #[cfg(test)] module tests for coverage gates to remain meaningful

The full validation suite before a PR includes: xci-fast, integration tests, spec conformance, proptest state machines, insta snapshots, spec validate, snapshot-count lint, clippy, llvm-cov, and mutants

friction: Cargo rejected multiple test filters in a single chained command (cargo test filter1 filter2 is not valid syntax), requiring separate invocations
GraphQL thread query had a typo in one poll iteration, causing a silent miss on that polling round
lib-only coverage gate (--lib) did not capture the new integration tests, requiring a second round of unit-level tests to be added inside the modules to maintain coverage metrics
Codex review took 10+ minutes (5+ polling cycles at 2 min each) before returning findings
open_items: PR #682 is not yet merged — Codex review findings were addressed but the PR still needs final approval and CI to pass on the updated commit
Stat component was included in the PR title and summary but no review findings or fixes were mentioned for it — worth verifying its value sanitization is consistent with the meter/progress fixes
Mutation testing (cargo mutants) was listed in the original PR validation checklist but was not re-run after the review fixes were applied
